episodes.title: Reading Changed My Life // how I came to leave the cult I grew up in
episodes.description:
A few years after a massive scandal broke, I realized that I had grown up in a cult. It was a hard realization, but I had to come face to face with the data and research. After I had grown up believing many specific things about myself, womanhood, and marriage, I realized that things were no longer adding up and that I likely had believed some things that just weren't accurate. Here is a brief look at that process and how reading changed my life, giving me the chance to reclaim huge parts of myself.

I am sharing this to honor myself, honor my voice, and honor the other people in my family and circles who had their voices, desires, and intuition taken from them.

Here are some of the books that I read:
No Little Women by Aimee Byrd
Are Women Human? Penetrating, Sensible, and Witty Essays on the Role of Women in Society by Dorothy L Sayers
Jesus Feminist by Sarah Bessey
Prey Tell: Why We Silence Women Who Tell the Truth and How Everyone Can Speak Up by Tiffany Bluhm
The Making of Biblical Womanhood by Beth Allison Barr
Untwisting Scriptures (series) by Rebecca Davis
Beyond Authority and Submission: Women and Men in Marriage, Church, and Society by Aimee Byrd
Recovering from Biblical Manhood and Womanhood: How the Church Needs to Rediscover Her Purpose by Aimee Byrd
The Equality Workbook: Freedom in Christ from the Oppression of Patriarchy by Helga Edwards and Bob Edwards
The Blue Parakeet: Rethinking How you Read the Bible by Scot McKnight
